primary_legislation.all: Lifting Operations and Lifting Equipment Regulations 1998 (LOLER); Pressure Systems Safety Regulations 2000 (PSSR); Health and Safety at Work Act 1974
primary_legislation.northern_ireland: Lifting Operations and Lifting Equipment Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1999 (LOLER NI); Pressure Systems Safety Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2004

notes: Differences from school set: REMOVED — passenger lifts (most GP premises don't have them), PE equipment, SEN hoists. ADDED — height-adjustable power-operated examination couches (LOLER), patient/bariatric transfer hoists (LOLER, where present), autoclave/benchtop steriliser (PSSR), compressed oxygen and medical gas systems (PSSR), stair lifts (LOLER, where fitted for accessibility). Autoclaves are also covered in GP-MOD-029 (Decontamination).

logbooks.title: 6-Monthly LOLER Examination — Power-Operated Examination Couches and Hoists
logbooks.applicable_condition: Where the practice has power-operated (electric or hydraulic) height-adjustable examination couches or patient transfer hoists.
logbooks.frequency_detail: Every 6 months for equipment used to lift persons (LOLER Regulation 9(3)(a)). Manual (non-power) examination couches are not in scope for LOLER but should be maintained in accordance with manufacturer guidance.

steps.description: Arrange examination by a competent person (typically the equipment manufacturer's service engineer or an independent LOLER-competent inspector).

Inspector examines each power-operated couch or hoist: structural integrity, electrical/hydraulic systems, motor and actuator function, safety interlocks, remote controls, maximum safe working load (SWL) confirmation, emergency lowering mechanism.
Inspector provides a written examination report (LOLER Regulation 10). Any defect that is a risk to persons must be remedied immediately — the equipment must not be used until repaired.
steps.photo_guidance: Photograph the examination report and any defect identified.

steps.description: Retain the examination reports. Record: date, equipment reference, examiner name and competence, outcome, defects found, action taken, next examination due.

logbooks.title: 6-Monthly LOLER Examination — Stair Lift (if fitted)
logbooks.applicable_condition: Only where a stair lift is installed for accessibility (staff and/or patient use).
logbooks.frequency_detail: Every 6 months — stair lifts are used to carry persons and therefore require 6-monthly LOLER thorough examination.

steps.description: Arrange thorough examination with the stair lift manufacturer's service agent or a LOLER-competent inspector.
Inspector examines: carriage and seat assembly, safety edges and obstruction sensors, drive chain/rack, limit switches (top and bottom), safety cut-out device, call/send controls, SWL marking.
Inspector provides a written examination report. Any defect affecting safe use must be remedied before the lift is returned to service.
Record: date, stair lift reference, examiner, outcome, defects, action taken, next examination due.

logbooks.title: PSSR Written Scheme and Annual Inspection — Autoclave / Benchtop Steriliser
logbooks.applicable_condition: Where the practice has a benchtop autoclave / benchtop steriliser for instrument reprocessing. Where autoclaves are present, decontamination logbooks in GP-MOD-029 must also be maintained.
logbooks.frequency_detail: Annual PSSR inspection of the pressure vessel. Additionally, quarterly Bowie-Dick / helix tests are recorded in GP-MOD-029.

steps.description: Confirm a Written Scheme of Examination (WSE) is in place for the autoclave, prepared by a competent person (typically a Pressure Systems Safety Regulations-competent engineer or the equipment manufacturer). The WSE defines the scope and frequency of examinations.

Arrange the annual PSSR examination per the WSE. The examiner inspects: pressure vessel integrity, safety valve function and set pressure, pressure gauge accuracy, door seal and interlocks, control system, steam trap and discharge system.

In addition to the PSSR inspection, confirm the autoclave has an annual validation and annual maintenance service per HTM 01-05 (Decontamination in Primary and Community Dental Care — used as the standard for GP practices). See GP-MOD-029 for decontamination logbooks.
Examiner provides a written examination report. Any defect must be remedied before the autoclave is returned to service for instrument reprocessing.
Record: date, autoclave reference and serial number, examiner name and competence, WSE reference, outcome, defects, action taken, next examination due.

logbooks.title: PSSR — Medical Gas (Oxygen) Cylinder and Manifold System
logbooks.applicable_condition: Where the practice stores and uses compressed oxygen cylinders (e.g., for emergency use or for dispensing practices supplying domiciliary oxygen). Piped medical gas systems (if any) require a separate written scheme.
logbooks.frequency_detail: Annual check of storage, handling, and written scheme compliance for pressurised oxygen systems.

steps.description: Confirm a suitable Written Scheme of Examination is in place if the oxygen system exceeds the threshold requiring one under PSSR (most portable cylinder set-ups with a regulator fall below the PSSR threshold for a formal WSE, but good practice dictates documented procedures).

Inspect the storage area for oxygen cylinders: segregated from flammable gases and materials, secured upright (chained or in approved cylinder store), no smoking signage displayed, cylinders stored away from direct heat sources.
Check each cylinder: within test date (stamped on cylinder shoulder), valve protection cap fitted when not in use, no visible damage to cylinder body or valve.
Confirm regulators and flow metres are in good condition and within calibration/service date.
Confirm all staff who use oxygen are trained in correct assembly, use, and emergency procedures (including what to do if a fire occurs near a cylinder).
Record: date, cylinders inspected, storage conditions, regulator/flowmeter condition, staff training confirmed, any deficiencies, action taken.
